subject: ClickHouse Migration RFC — please review before Friday

messages.subject: ClickHouse Migration RFC — please review before Friday
messages.body:
Liam, Jordan,

I've uploaded the ClickHouse migration RFC to Drive (Engineering/Specs). Can you both review before Friday's sync? I want a go/no-go call by end of next week.

Two things I most want your eyes on:
1. Schema design — the event_metadata column type decision (LowCardinality vs Nullable). I've proposed LowCardinality with null coercion on ingest, but I want Liam to sanity-check the write path.
2. Retention change — moving from 180 to 90 days raw. Jordan already signed off on this from a cost perspective; want to make sure Liam is aligned.

Priya is CC'd for visibility — no action needed from her.

-M

messages.subject: Re: ClickHouse Migration RFC — please review before Friday
messages.body:
Reviewed. Two things:

1. LowCardinality for event_metadata is correct. One thing to watch: if cardinality exceeds ~10K distinct values, ClickHouse silently degrades it to a regular String. We should add a cardinality monitor post-launch.
2. The ZSTD compression config says 'default' — I'd specify ZSTD(3) explicitly. The default has changed across ClickHouse versions and I don't want a silent regression after a Cloud update.

Everything else looks solid. +1 to proceed.

— L

messages.subject: Re: ClickHouse Migration RFC — please review before Friday
messages.body:
Staging cluster is up in us-east-1. I'm running a replay of last week's Brainloop traffic against it and write latency looks good — p99 is 18ms at 52M events/day.

One flag: ClickHouse Cloud is quoting $800/mo for the tier I provisioned. That's roughly double the TimescaleDB EC2 instance. I know we projected this — just flagging so nobody is surprised when the first invoice lands.

— J

messages.subject: Re: ClickHouse Migration RFC — please review before Friday
messages.body:
Good catches both. Liam — updating RFC to ZSTD(3) explicitly and adding a cardinality monitor note. Jordan — the $800/mo is accounted for in the infra cost review from Oct 20. Break-even vs. TimescaleDB is around 45M events/day, which we'll hit by January.

Green light to proceed. Phase 1 starts next week. Jordan, can you have staging fully validated by Nov 22?

-M
